data.title: Michigan sues oil companies for collusion against renewables, EVs
data.description:
Michigan's attorney general filed an antitrust lawsuit against four major oil companies Friday, claiming they have colluded for decades to reduce production and distribution of renewable energy and electric vehicles.

The lawsuit was filed in U.S. District Court in western Michigan and named BP (BP), Chevron (CVX), Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Shell (SHEL), as well as the American Petroleum Institute, saying the group acted "as a cartel, agreeing to reduce the production and distribution of electricity from renewable sources and to restrain the emergence of electric vehicles and renewable primary energy technologies in the United States."

The lawsuit suit noted that in the 1970s, Exxon (XOM) developed the first hybrid gas-electric vehicle technologies, publicly showed an electric motor integrated into a hybrid gas-electric propulsion system installed in a Chrysler Cordoba, and partnered with Toyota to develop a hybrid gas-electric vehicle using a Toyota Cressida chassis.

"But Exxon never marketed that innovative hybrid engine technology and consistently has deferred meaningful investment in its lithium-ion and graphite-based battery technologies for EVs," the lawsuit said.

The lawsuit also alleged Chevron (CVX) sought to delay a critical EV and battery technology known as nickel-metal hydride rechargeable batteries by acquiring patents to restrict their use in automobiles.

data.title: Buying frenzy lifts gold near $5,000, silver past $100 as hedges against rising geopolitical risk
data.description:
Gold futures rallied to another record, pushing closer to the $5K/oz milestone on rising geopolitical risks, economic uncertainty and a weaker U.S. dollar, while silver futures surged past $100/oz for the first time.

Gold has now gained 15% this month alone and 79% in the past year, while silver has soared 44% in January and 225% from a year ago.

Although geopolitical tensions have eased slightly after President Trump ruled out using military force to annex Greenland from Denmark, his threats have forced some European investment firms and pension funds to revalue their U.S. bond holdings.

Analysts say gold increasingly looks like a hedge against President Trump's unpredictable actions, and while many traders had framed gold as a hedge against the risk of a U.S.-Europe tariff war amid Trump's pursuit of Greenland, gold's gains have not slowed despite the removal of the tariff threat.

"Momentum has clearly become part of the story, with FOMO [fear of missing out] playing a visible role as prices push into uncharted territory," Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen said in a note. "Yet it would be a mistake to dismiss the rally as purely speculative."

"Central bank demand remains firm, the dollar continues to weaken, and governments keep issuing debt with little clarity on long-term repayment," Hansen wrote.

Silver also is enjoying support from strong physical demand in China, although high prices could start hurting consumption in the long run, Hansen said, noting that local futures prices continue to trade at a premium of more than $12/oz over London prices, pointing to tight regional availability and strong appetite in spite of surging global prices.

"The risk of demand destruction cannot be ignored if prices accelerate too far, too fast - a dynamic that could eventually favor a rotation back toward gold," the analyst said.

For the first time in 14 years, it would take just 50 oz of silver to buy an ounce of gold, down from 105 oz in April, a ratio that normally would provide a warning that silver's outperformance over gold has become stretched.

On Friday, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +1.4% to $4,976.20/oz, and front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +5.1% to $100.925/oz; for the week, gold gained 8.4% and silver surged 14.6%, the largest weekly percentage gains since March 2020 and July 2020, respectively.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Sarepta jumps on upcoming release of 3-year Elevidys data
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) is up ~10% in after-hours trading Friday after announcing it will release topline, 3-year results from its EMBARK study of Elevidys (delandistrogene moxeparvovec) in Duchenne muscular dystrophy the morning of Jan. 26.  
  • The data from part 1 is in ambulatory patients with Duchenne who were four to seven years old at time of treatment.
  • The company will hold a webcast and conference call at 830a ET on Jan. 26.

data.title: Jack Nathan Medical names interim CEO as Marchelletta steps down
data.description:
• Jack Nathan Medical (JNHMF) on Friday said that Chief Executive Officer and Vice-Chairman Mike Marchelletta has stepped down effective immediately and will lead a prospective investor syndicate evaluating a potential strategic transaction involving the company.
  • The company appointed Michael Di Cesare, its former co-founder and Chief Operating Officer, as interim CEO and Vice-Chairman.
  • Jack Nathan said Marchelletta has agreed to a four-month lock-up covering 21 million common shares and will temporarily assign voting rights on those shares to Di Cesare during the lock-up period.
  • Marchelletta will also continue to defer executive compensation, the company said.
  • The company said the leadership transition comes as part of broader restructuring and balance sheet optimization efforts, which have included eliminating approximately $15 million in debt and cancelling about 140 million common shares previously issuable under legacy debenture arrangements.
  • Jack Nathan said there is no assurance that any strategic transaction will be completed.
  • JNH:CA closed -0.0% at $0.01.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zoomcar announces launch of offer to exchange outstanding warrants for common stock
data.description:
• Zoomcar Holdings (ZCAR) on Friday announced the commencement of a voluntary offer to exchange several series of its outstanding warrants for shares of the company’s common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • Simultaneously, the company announced the launch of a bridge financing of up to $5M, with a minimum required offering amount of $2M, to be conducted as a private placement.
  • The company is making the offer to exchange as part of a broader effort to simplify its capital structure by reducing the number of outstanding warrant instruments and consolidating its equity capitalization. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the Bridge Financing for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CN Energy receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• CN Energy (NASDAQ:CNEY) announced on Friday that it received a notice from Nasdaq determining to delist the company’s Class A ordinary shares for noncompliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • Pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5810(c)(3)(A)(iv), the company is not eligible for a compliance period or extension because it carried out multiple reverse stock splits within the past 3 years.
  • Nasdaq noted the company carried out a 1-for-30 reverse stock split in January 2024 and a 1-for-25 split in May 2025, resulting in a cumulative 1-for-750 reverse split of its Class A shares.
  • On Jan. 21, 2026, the company submitted a hearing request to appeal Nasdaq’s delisting determination and plans to present a plan seeking an exception or other relief to address the identified deficiencies.
  • Shares +64.30%.

data.title: Nexalin Technology receives Nasdaq bid price deficiency notice
data.description:
• Nexalin Technology (NXL) received a Nasdaq notification dated January 21, 2026, stating it is not in compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • Nexalin has been granted a 180-day compliance period until July 20, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The company is evaluating operational and financial measures to improve its share price and is not considering a reverse stock split at this time.

data.title: Cathay General Bancorp announces CFO retirement, leadership transition
data.description:
• Cathay General Bancorp (CATY) chief financial officer and treasurer Heng W. Chen will retire effective March 1, 2026.
  • Deputy CFO Albert J. Wang will succeed Chen as CFO of Cathay and treasurer of Cathay General Bancorp.
  • Chen will remain a special advisor to the office of the president at Cathay Bank through December 31, 2026, to support the transition.

data.title: Basel Medical receives delinquency notification from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Basel Medical Group (NASDAQ:BMGL) said it received a Nasdaq notice for failing to meet the $1 minimum bid price requirement after 30 consecutive trading days below the level.
  • The company has 180 days to regain compliance by maintaining a closing bid price of at least $1 for 10 consecutive business days, including a potential reverse stock split before July 21, 2026.
  • The company said it is working with advisers to prepare a plan to address the listing non-compliance.\
  • Shares +2.54%.

data.title: U.S. rig count bumps higher in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of oil and gas rigs actively drilling in the U.S. added 1 to 544 during the past week, according to the latest survey reported Friday by Baker Hughes.

During the week ended January 23, drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 1 to 411, while gas rigs were unchanged at 122, and 11 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down by 32, or 5.5%, from the same time a year ago.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin, Eagle Ford and Williston Basin all stayed flat for the week at 244, 29, and 28, respectively.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
